# `lang.jcl.runtime.zos` — IBM z/OS JCL (JES2/JES3)

Auto-generated. Back to [summary](../summary.md).

- **Language:** [JCL](../by-language/jcl.md)
- **Category:** [language](../by-category/language.md)
- **Capability cells:** 5

## Capabilities

| Capability | Status | Verified at | Issue | Cites | Notes |
|------------|--------|-------------|-------|-------|-------|
| Call line precision | ✅ `full` | `2026-06-12` | 2843 | `internal/extractors/jcl/extractor.go`<br>`internal/extractors/jcl/extractor_test.go` | Every CALLS edge carries Properties["line"] (1-based physical line of the EXEC card): EXEC PGM=, EXEC PROC=/positional proc, and the recovered TSO CALL edge all stamp the line. Proven by TestExtractor_ExecPgmCallsEdge / TestExtractor_TSOCallEdge. |
| Core extraction | ✅ `full` | `2026-06-14` | 2843 | `internal/extractors/jcl/extractor.go`<br>`internal/extractors/jcl/extractor_test.go`<br>`internal/extractors/jcl/testdata/condflow.jcl`<br>`internal/extractors/jcl/testdata/payjob.jcl` | Line-oriented card parser (no tree-sitter JCL grammar; mirrors cobol/verilog precedent). joinStatements collapses col-72-bounded continuation cards into logical statements (trailing-comma signal). Verb switch emits: //NAME JOB → SCOPE.Component/job; //STEP EXEC → SCOPE.Operation/step; //NAME PROC..PEND → SCOPE.Component/proc (inline-proc scope tracking); //DD DSN= → SCOPE.Datastore/dataset. CONTAINS wires job/proc → their steps and step → dataset (ownerForStep follows the open inline PROC, else the JOB). Operand-only (nameless) cards are re-shifted name→verb via isStatementKeyword so `//  INCLUDE MEMBER=X` parses correctly. NEW in #5044 — conditional-flow modelling (reuses PRECEDES + step Properties, no new Kinds): (a) COND=(code,op[,step]) on EXEC records cond/cond_step on the step and emits a conditional PRECEDES edge FROM the referenced prior step (or the immediately-preceding step, cond_scope=all_prior, when no step is named) TO this step with flow=conditional cond_kind=COND cond_code/cond_op props; COND=EVEN/ONLY abnormal-termination forms record cond=EVEN/ONLY. (b) `// IF (cond) THEN ... // ELSE ... // ENDIF` tags every EXEC step declared inside the block with if_cond + if_branch (then/else) — the IF/ELSE/ENDIF cards themselves emit no entity. (c) RESTART=step on the JOB records restart=step on the JOB and flags steps before the entry point restart_skipped=true and the entry step restart_entry=true. Proven by TestExtractor_JobAndSteps / _ProcDefinition / _Datasets / _LanguageTagged / _CondStepSkip / _IfElseEndif / _Restart / _CondFlowNoOp (no-match no-op) / _CondFlowWrongLanguageNoOp (COBOL yields no JCL flow). |
| Fs effect | ✅ `full` | `2026-06-14` | 5043 | `internal/extractors/jcl/extractor.go`<br>`internal/extractors/jcl/extractor_test.go`<br>`internal/extractors/jcl/testdata/multidsn.jcl` | DD statements with a DSN=/DSNAME= operand emit a SCOPE.Datastore/dataset entity (ddDsnRe) and a step→dataset data-flow edge keyed off the DD disposition: DISP=NEW/MOD → WRITES_TO, DISP=OLD/SHR/default → READS_FROM. DSN-less DDs (SYSOUT/DUMMY/instream `*`) are skipped. #4944: a PGM=IDCAMS step has its SYSIN REPRO/IMPORT/EXPORT IN/FROMDATASET / OUT/TODATASET control cards scanned (scanControlCards/idcamsRepro*Re) to recover the literal-DSN dataset I/O the bare DD cards do not attribute — READS_FROM the source, WRITES_TO the target, via="IDCAMS", with a dataset entity emitted for each so the edge target is not an orphan (TestExtractor_IDCAMSReproDatasets). Proven by TestExtractor_Datasets (PROD.PAYROLL.MASTER read via DISP=SHR, PROD.PAYROLL.RESULTS written via DISP=NEW). NEW in #5043 — multi-DSN-per-DD granularity: (a) CONCATENATED DDs — a named DD followed by nameless `//  DD DSN=...` continuation cards now emits a dataset entity + read edge per concatenant, all carrying Properties["ddname"]=<logical DD>; the continuants are flagged concatenated=true with concatenation_position (TestExtractor_ConcatenatedDD). (b) GDG relative generations — DSN=BASE(+1)/(0)/(-1) (gdgGenRe) resolves to the BASE generation-data-group entity with gdg_base / gdg_generation props on the entity and the data-flow edge; a (+n) next-generation create is treated as a WRITE even absent DISP=NEW (TestExtractor_GDGRelativeGeneration). (c) PDS member granularity — DSN=LIB(MEMBER) (pdsMemberRe) keeps the granular LIB(MEMBER) entity identity distinct from the bare library, with pds_library / pds_member props (TestExtractor_PDSMember). Plain single-DSN DDs carry none of these flags (TestExtractor_MultiDSNNoOp). Remaining (follow-up): IDCAMS INFILE/OUTFILE DD-reference forms still surface only via their DD cards (the literal IN/OUTDATASET forms are recovered). |
| Import resolution quality | ✅ `full` | `2026-06-14` | 5042 | `internal/extractors/jcl/extractor.go`<br>`internal/extractors/jcl/extractor_test.go`<br>`internal/extractors/jcl/testdata/symjob.jcl` | Cross-file/cross-language link kinds, all bound by the by-name resolver with no new linker code. (1) The JCL→COBOL bridge (#2843): EXEC PGM=<prog> emits CALLS via="EXEC PGM" external=true cross_language=cobol whose bare ToID binds to the sibling COBOL PROGRAM-ID — proven end-to-end by TestCrossLanguageBridge_JCLtoCOBOL (resolves PAYROLL across internal/extractors/jcl/testdata/payjob.jcl ↔ ../cobol/testdata/payroll.cbl). (2) #4907: //  INCLUDE MEMBER=<name> emits IMPORTS import_kind=include (TestExtractor_IncludeImports). (3) #4907: a TSO terminal-monitor step (PGM=IKJEFT01/IKJEFT1B/IKJEFT1A) has its SYSTSIN `CALL 'lib(MEMBER)'` control card scanned to recover the indirect JCL→program edge — via="TSO CALL" host_program=IKJEFTxx (TestExtractor_TSOCallEdge). (4) #4944: the SYSIN/SYSTSIN scanner is generalised from CALL-only to a per-utility control-card grammar (scanControlCards). A DB2 batch step (IKJEFT01 or PGM=DSNUTILB) with a `DSN SYSTEM(ssid) ... RUN PROGRAM(p) PLAN(pl)` control card emits CALLS via="DSN RUN PROGRAM" external=true cross_language=cobol db2_plan=pl db2_system=ssid host_program=<util> — the JCL→DB2/COBOL application-program edge the shell utility hides (TestExtractor_DSNRunProgramEdge, TestExtractor_DSNUTILBRunProgram). EXEC PROC=/positional-proc invocation also emits CALLS via="EXEC PROC". (5) NEW in #5042 — symbolic-parameter substitution so the bridge/dataset edge binds the SUBSTITUTED identity, not the literal &VAR token (which would break COBOL by-name binding and dataset identity): a job-level `// SET SYM=VAL[,SYM2=VAL2]` card and a PROC statement's SYMBOL=default operands seed a case-insensitive symbol table (symbolTable.learnPairs / learnDefaults); an `EXEC proc,SYMBOL=override` overrides PROC defaults (fill-if-absent precedence: SET/EXEC-override beats PROC default). Before any CALLS/READS_FROM/WRITES_TO edge is emitted, &SYM references in PGM=/PROC=/DSN= operands are resolved (symbolRefRe/substitute) — including the `&HLQ..FILE` concatenation-period form (the period after a resolved symbol is consumed) and multi-symbol `&HLQ..&DSNAME` operands. The pre-substitution literal is recorded as Properties["symbolic_source"]; an unknown symbol is left intact and flagged Properties["symbolic_unresolved"]="true". `// JCLLIB ORDER=(LIB1,LIB2)` is recorded as Properties["jcllib_order"] (normalised comma-joined proc-resolution search-order) on the JOB. Proven by TestExtractor_SetSymbolSubstitution (PGM=&PGMNAME→PAYROLL bridges to COBOL), _DsnSymbolSubstitution (&HLQ..PAYROLL.MASTER→PROD.PAYROLL.MASTER), _ProcSymbolDefaultAndOverride (EXEC DSNAME=ARCHIVE override beats PROC DSNAME=DEFLT), _UnresolvedSymbol (&UNDEF flagged), _JCLLibOrder, _NoSymbolsNoOp (no-match no-op), _WrongLanguageNoOp (COBOL source yields no JCL entities). Follow-ups: #5044 (COND=/IF-THEN-ELSE-ENDIF/RESTART= conditional-flow edges) now delivered — see core_extraction; %CLIST under TSO deferred. KNOWN LIMITATION (#5042): the symbol table is job-wide rather than scoped per-PROC-invocation (correct for the common single-invocation case; nested/multiply-invoked procs with conflicting symbol values are a future refinement). |
| Navigates to | 🟢 `partial` | `2026-06-24` | 5369 | `internal/extractors/jcl/extractor.go`<br>`internal/extractors/jcl/extractor_test.go`<br>`internal/extractors/jcl/testdata/payjob.jcl` | PARTIAL (mainframe-migration lineage spike #5369) — the full JCL job->program->dataset lineage chain: a JOB CONTAINS its EXEC steps, an EXEC PGM= step emits a bare-name CALLS edge (cross_language=cobol) the by-name resolver binds to the COBOL PROGRAM-ID (the orchestration->implementation bridge, mainframe analog of the HTTP cross-repo linker), and DD DSN= cards bind datasets with DISP-governed READS_FROM/WRITES_TO direction. Honest-partial: bare-name program/dataset binding; no STEPLIB load-library disambiguation, catalog resolution, or GDG absolute-generation expansion. See TestMainframeLineageSpike_JobPgmDataset / TestCrossLanguageBridge_JCLtoCOBOL. |
